title.en: Why Does Our Family Use Two Languages?
title.zh: 为什么我们家会说两种话？

knowledge_outline: Families use languages to connect with people and places.
Using two languages can help a child understand more words and stories.
Switching languages is a normal part of many bilingual homes.

fact_claims.claim: Many families use more than one language at home to communicate with different people and communities.
fact_claims.source_note: Early childhood bilingual-family guidance used for seed content only.

script.en: At breakfast, one family says good morning and zao an at the same table.
script.zh: 早餐桌上，一家人一会儿说早安，一会儿说 good morning。

question.en: Why might a family use two languages?
question.zh: 一家人为什么会用两种语言呢？

options.en: One language is for weekdays only.
options.zh: 一种语言只能周一到周五用。

options.en: Different words help us talk with different people.
options.zh: 不同的词能帮我们和不同的人说话。

script.en: Leo called Grandma and said, ni hao. Then he told his teacher, good morning. His mom said languages are like keys. One key opens a chat with Grandma. Another key opens a chat at school. In their home, both keys belong on the same ring.
script.zh: 乐乐给奶奶打电话，说：你好。到学校见到老师，他又说：good morning。妈妈说，语言像钥匙。一把钥匙打开和奶奶聊天的门，一把钥匙打开和学校聊天的门。在我们家，两把钥匙都挂在同一个钥匙圈上。

question.en: When would you choose Chinese, and when would you choose English?
question.zh: 什么时候你会选中文，什么时候会选英文？

answer_guidance.en: The child may name family, school, books, songs, or friends.
answer_guidance.zh: 孩子可以说家人、学校、书、歌或者朋友。

prompt.en: Tell your grown-up one reason two languages can help.
prompt.zh: 告诉大人：两种语言有什么用？

prompt.en: What other word do you know in two languages?
prompt.zh: 你还知道哪个词有两种说法？

recall_question.en: Why did Leo's family use two languages?
recall_question.zh: 乐乐家为什么用两种语言？

answer_hint.en: Different languages helped them talk with different people.
answer_hint.zh: 不同语言帮他们和不同的人聊天。
